summary: Operate the DynamoDB tables: toggle point-in-time recovery and TTL, switch billing mode, resize provisioned capacity, tag, guard with deletion protection, and delete a table with engineer review.

operations.title: Toggle point-in-time recovery
operations.summary: Turn point-in-time recovery on or off, letting you restore to any moment in the last 35 days. Turning it off removes that safety net.
operations.description: Enables or disables continuous backups (PITR) on the table, allowing restore to any second in the trailing 35 days. Enabling has no downtime and a modest storage cost; disabling removes that restore capability going forward.

params.name: table
params.label: DynamoDB table
params.help: Select the target DynamoDB table.

params.name: new_point_in_time_recovery_enabled
params.label: Enable PITR
params.help: Turn continuous point-in-time backups on or off.

operations.title: Configure item expiry (TTL)
operations.summary: Turn on automatic expiry of old items and pick which field holds the expiry time. Deletion runs in the background within about two days.
operations.description: Enables or disables Time to Live and sets the item attribute DynamoDB reads as the expiry epoch timestamp, so stale rows (sessions, queue entries) are auto-purged. Deletion is background and eventually consistent, typically within 48 hours of expiry.

params.name: table
params.label: DynamoDB table
params.help: Select the target DynamoDB table.

params.name: new_ttl_enabled
params.label: Enable TTL
params.help: Turn automatic item expiry on or off.

params.name: new_ttl_attribute_name
params.label: TTL attribute name
params.help: Item attribute holding the expiry time as a Unix epoch (seconds).

operations.title: Switch billing mode (on-demand / provisioned)
operations.summary: Switch this table between on-demand and fixed-capacity billing. Fixed capacity needs units set first. It applies live with no downtime.
operations.description: Moves the table between PAY_PER_REQUEST and PROVISIONED capacity modes. This is a live, no-downtime change, but switching to PROVISIONED requires read/write capacity to be set (see the resize-capacity operation) or the table will default to minimal throughput.

params.name: table
params.label: DynamoDB table
params.help: Select the target DynamoDB table.

params.name: new_billing_mode
params.label: Billing mode
params.help: PAY_PER_REQUEST scales automatically and bills per request; PROVISIONED uses fixed read/write capacity units.

operations.title: Resize provisioned read/write capacity
operations.summary: Change this table's read and write capacity to relieve throttling or cut cost. Applies live; only affects fixed-capacity tables.
operations.description: Adjusts read_capacity and write_capacity units on a PROVISIONED table to relieve throttling or reduce spend. Applies live with no downtime; has no effect on PAY_PER_REQUEST tables.

params.name: table
params.label: DynamoDB table
params.help: Select the target DynamoDB table (must be in PROVISIONED billing mode).

params.name: new_read_capacity
params.label: Read capacity units
params.help: Provisioned read capacity units (RCU).

params.name: new_write_capacity
params.label: Write capacity units
params.help: Provisioned write capacity units (WCU).

operations.title: Add or update a table tag
operations.summary: Add or update a tag on this table, for example for cost tracking or ownership. Metadata only, no effect on table availability.
operations.description: Sets a key/value tag on the table for cost allocation, ownership or compliance tracking (e.g. Environment, Service, DataClass). Live change, no impact to table availability.

params.name: table
params.label: DynamoDB table
params.help: Select the target DynamoDB table.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: Tag name, e.g. CostCentre or DataClass.

params.name: tag_value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: Value to assign to this tag.

operations.title: Toggle deletion protection
operations.summary: Turn this table's deletion protection on or off. Off removes a safety net just before deleting it.
operations.description: Enables or disables deletion_protection_enabled, which blocks the table from being deleted (via console, API or Terraform) while turned on. Disabling it removes a safety guardrail ahead of an intentional table removal.

params.name: table
params.label: DynamoDB table
params.help: Select the target DynamoDB table.

params.name: new_deletion_protection_enabled
params.label: Enable deletion protection
params.help: When on, the table cannot be deleted until this is turned off.

operations.title: Delete a DynamoDB table
operations.summary: Permanently delete this table and all its items. This can't be undone; apps still writing to it will fail. An engineer reviews it first.
operations.description: Permanently removes the table and all its items; there is no undo once applied, and any application still writing to it will fail. Requires deletion_protection_enabled to be off first and explicit confirmation of data loss.

params.name: table
params.label: DynamoDB table
params.help: Select the table to delete.

params.name: confirm_data_loss
params.label: Confirm permanent data loss
params.help: Must be explicitly confirmed; all items in the table will be permanently lost.

operations.title: Change DynamoDB table class
operations.summary: Switch this table between standard and infrequent-access pricing. AWS allows only two class changes every 30 days. Reviewed by an approver.
operations.description: Switch table_class between STANDARD and STANDARD_INFREQUENT_ACCESS. In-place, reversible, no downtime; changes storage vs throughput pricing. AWS limits table-class changes to twice per rolling 30-day window, so this is gated with guardrails.

params.name: table
params.label: Table
params.help: Select the DynamoDB table whose table_class changes; the inventory picker limits the change to estate-managed tables.

params.name: table_class
params.label: Table class
params.help: Sets table_class: STANDARD is the default price mix, while STANDARD_INFREQUENT_ACCESS trades cheaper storage for higher per-request cost and suits rarely-read data; the allowlist holds the only two classes AWS supports, so no invalid value can be submitted.

operations.title: Configure DynamoDB Streams
operations.summary: Turn this table's change stream on or off. Disabling it can break consumers like Lambda functions.
operations.description: Enable or disable DynamoDB Streams and set the stream view type together (view type is only valid while streams are enabled). In-place, reversible, no downtime. Disabling can break stream consumers such as Lambda or Kinesis, so this is guardrailed.

params.name: table
params.label: Table
params.help: Select the DynamoDB table whose Streams configuration changes; the inventory picker limits the change to estate-managed tables.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Streams enabled
params.help: Sets stream_enabled: true publishes every item change to a DynamoDB Stream for consumers such as Lambda or Kinesis, false stops the stream and can break those consumers, which is why this operation is guardrailed; only true/false are accepted.

params.name: view_type
params.label: Stream view type
params.help: Applies when streams are enabled; ignored when disabling.

operations.title: Toggle server-side encryption (AWS-managed key)
operations.summary: Switch this table's encryption key between AWS-managed and the account default. Data stays encrypted either way. Reviewed by an approver.
operations.description: Enable or disable server_side_encryption using the AWS-managed KMS key (aws/dynamodb). In-place, reversible, no downtime. Disabling reduces encryption-at-rest posture; supplying a customer-managed CMK ARN is engineer-authored via the catch-all.

params.name: table
params.label: Table
params.help: Select the DynamoDB table whose server-side encryption setting changes; the inventory picker limits the change to estate-managed tables.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Encryption enabled
params.help: Sets server_side_encryption.enabled: true encrypts the table with the AWS-managed KMS key (aws/dynamodb), false falls back to DynamoDB's default AWS-owned key; data stays encrypted at rest under both values, so the toggle only changes which key is used.

operations.title: Set point-in-time recovery window
operations.summary: Set how many days back you can restore with point-in-time recovery, from 1 to 35. Requires point-in-time recovery to already be turned on.
operations.description: Set point_in_time_recovery.recovery_period_in_days, the PITR restore window in days. In-place, reversible, no downtime. Requires PITR already enabled (toggle handled separately).

params.name: table
params.label: Table
params.help: Select the DynamoDB table whose point-in-time-recovery window changes; the table must already have PITR enabled, and the inventory picker limits the change to estate-managed tables.

params.name: days
params.label: Recovery period (days)
params.help: Valid range 1–35 days.

operations.title: Set on-demand throughput caps
operations.summary: Cap this on-demand table's maximum read and write throughput to guard against runaway cost. No downtime.
operations.description: Set on_demand_throughput max_read_request_units and max_write_request_units for PAY_PER_REQUEST tables. In-place, reversible, no downtime; caps guard against runaway cost. Guardrailed because it affects a table's capacity ceiling.

params.name: table
params.label: Table
params.help: Select the PAY_PER_REQUEST table whose on-demand throughput caps change; the inventory picker limits the change to estate-managed tables, and the caps only bound spend, they store no data.

params.name: max_read_request_units
params.label: Max read request units
params.help: Upper bound on on-demand read units (1–40000).

params.name: max_write_request_units
params.label: Max write request units
params.help: Upper bound on on-demand write units (1–40000).

operations.title: Remove a tag
operations.summary: Remove a tag from this table. Metadata only, no effect on table availability.
operations.description: Remove a single tags map entry by key. Cosmetic metadata change, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: table
params.label: Table
params.help: Select the DynamoDB table to remove the tag from; the inventory picker keeps this metadata-only change on estate-managed tables, and a removed tag can always be re-added.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: Key of the tag entry to remove.

operations.title: Request another change to DynamoDB table
operations.summary: Request any other change to this table, like renaming it, changing keys or indexes, or adding replicas. An engineer writes the Terraform.
operations.description: Catch-all for any change to a DynamoDB table not offered as a bounded self-service form — e.g. renaming, changing keys or indexes (replacement), customer-managed KMS keys, replicas/global tables, or S3 import/restore. An engineer authors the Terraform.

params.name: table
params.label: Table
params.help: The table to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: purpose
params.label: What change do you need?
params.help: Anything not offered as a bounded form — an engineer authors the Terraform.

operations.title: Set warm throughput (read/write units per second)
operations.summary: Set this table's pre-provisioned throughput. Raising it is live; lowering it replaces the table, destroying its data. Reviewed first.
operations.description: Sets warm_throughput.read_units_per_second and warm_throughput.write_units_per_second on the table — the pre-provisioned instantaneous read/write capacity for predictable high-traffic tables. Increasing either value applies in-place with no downtime; DECREASING either value below its current setting forces DynamoDB to recreate the base table (data loss), per the provider doc, so this is engineer-authored and reviewed. Provider minimums (confirmed against the upstream aws provider doc): read_units_per_second >= 12000, write_units_per_second >= 4000.

params.name: table
params.label: DynamoDB table
params.help: Select the target DynamoDB table.

params.name: read_units_per_second
params.label: Warm read units per second
params.help: Pre-provisioned instantaneous read capacity. Provider minimum 12000; decreasing below the current value forces table replacement.

params.name: write_units_per_second
params.label: Warm write units per second
params.help: Pre-provisioned instantaneous write capacity. Provider minimum 4000; decreasing below the current value forces table replacement.

operations.downtime: none if increased; table replacement if decreased

operations.title: Provision a new DynamoDB table
operations.summary: Request a brand-new table; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new table as a complete specification: billing mode, key schema, backup and encryption posture. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: table_name
params.label: Table name
params.help: Letters, numbers, underscore, dash and dot, 3-255 chars. Names the table in AWS and in the generated draft.

params.name: billing_mode
params.label: Billing mode
params.help: PAY_PER_REQUEST scales automatically and bills per request; PROVISIONED uses fixed read/write capacity units below.

params.name: read_capacity
params.label: Read capacity units
params.help: Provisioned read capacity units (RCU). Only applies in PROVISIONED billing mode.

params.name: write_capacity
params.label: Write capacity units
params.help: Provisioned write capacity units (WCU). Only applies in PROVISIONED billing mode.

params.name: hash_key
params.label: Partition key
params.help: The item attribute DynamoDB uses to partition data, e.g. id.

params.name: range_key
params.label: Sort key (optional)
params.help: Leave blank for a partition-key-only table. Set this to give the table a composite key.

params.name: attribute_definitions
params.label: Key attribute types
params.help: The type (S/N/B) declaration for each key above, and for any index added later — an engineer adds these to match.

params.name: pitr_enabled
params.label: Point-in-time recovery
params.help: Turn continuous point-in-time backups on or off.

params.name: kms_key
params.label: Encryption key
params.help: Only set this for a customer-managed key; blank uses DynamoDB's default AWS-owned key.

params.name: sse_enabled
params.label: Customer-managed encryption
params.help: Set automatically once an encryption key is chosen above — never asked directly.

params.name: deletion_protection_enabled
params.label: Deletion protection
params.help: When on, the table cannot be deleted until this is turned off.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Table name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this table holds
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this table.

decisions: The attribute type-declaration block(s) for the partition key, sort key, and any indexes are an engineer decision
Global and local secondary indexes are an engineer decision — this form provisions the base table only
Streams, TTL, and S3 import/restore are engineer follow-ups after creation
